summary: First-iteration E2E testing for CarDost ecommerce. Backend pytest suite (24/24 PASS) covers products, auth, guest+user checkout w/ mock payment, admin CRUD/stats, contact. Frontend Playwright covers home, shop+filter+search, product detail, cart, guest checkout (mock dialog), signup, login, logout, admin dashboard (stats/orders). Two real frontend bugs found.

ui_bugs.issue: Logged-in user's Full Name & Email are NOT prefilled in checkout form. useState initialiser runs once when AuthContext is still loading (user=null), so form starts empty even after /auth/me resolves. Missing useEffect to sync form.full_name / form.email when `user` becomes available. Repro: signup -> add to cart -> /checkout -> Full Name & Email fields are blank.

When POST /api/admin/products returns 422 (FastAPI validation error), `detail` is an array of objects but the UI passes it directly to toast/render. Causes React crash: 'Objects are not valid as a React child (found: object with keys {type, loc, msg, input, url})'. Should map detail[].msg or fall back to a generic message.

issues: Several 404 console errors observed on Home/Shop/Detail (likely missing favicon/static asset or image). Functional impact: none, but pollutes console.

action_items: Fix Checkout.jsx: add useEffect([user]) to setForm({ ...form, full_name: user?.name || form.full_name, email: user?.email || form.email }) when user loads, OR initialize lazily with the auth-loading guard.

Fix AdminDashboard.jsx error handling: when e.response?.data?.detail is an array, map it: `Array.isArray(d) ? d.map(x => x.msg).join(', ') : d`. Apply same defensive handling everywhere toast.error(detail) is called.
Optional: ensure favicon / static assets exist to silence 404 console noise.
critical_code_review_comments: Checkout form state bug (above) is a common pattern issue — applies any time you derive initial state from async-loaded context.

Backend /api/orders/verify accepts any order_id without auth and without rate-limiting. With MOCK_PAYMENT=true this is fine for demo; once MOCK_PAYMENT=false this branch is bypassed by signature check. Acceptable as scoped.
Server-side total computation correctly uses DB prices (good). Order creation does NOT decrement product stock — confirm if that's intentional.
JWT secret is in .env (good). Token TTL 30 days — acceptable for ecommerce but consider refresh tokens later.
/api/orders/{oid} is unauthenticated — anyone with order_id can read the order (includes address PII). Consider requiring matching user or signed link for guest.
test_credentials: Admin admin@cardost.com / Admin@123. Test users created on the fly during tests (TEST_user_*@cardost-test.com / Test@12345 and uitest*@cardost-test.com / Test@1234).
seed_data_creation: Backend creates 16 seed products + 1 admin on startup. Tests create additional users and orders (prefixed TEST_ where possible). One TEST_Admin_Product is created and deleted within pytest. 5 paid orders now exist in DB from this run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_cardost_api.py is stable and idempotent (unique email per run). Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env. MOCK_PAYMENT=true is honoured (mock dialog + simulated /verify). DB now has 5 paid orders + several test users from this run; admin stats reflect non-zero revenue. After main agent fixes the two UI bugs, re-test (a) logged-in checkout prefill and order-success flow, (b) Admin > Add Product happy-path AND validation error path (no React crash on 422).
rca of the issue: Bug 1 (Checkout prefill): React useState(initialValue) is only evaluated on mount; AuthContext fetches the user via /auth/me asynchronously inside a useEffect, so when Checkout mounts, user is null and form fields default to empty strings. Mitigation: add `useEffect(() => { if (user) setForm(f => ({ ...f, full_name: f.full_name || user.name, email: f.email || user.email })) }, [user])`. Bug 2 (Admin 422 render): FastAPI returns `detail` as an array of validation error dicts for Pydantic failures, but the error path passes it to React (likely via toast or inline render). Mitigation: normalize the detail to string: `const msg = Array.isArray(d) ? d.map(x => x.msg).join(', ') : (typeof d === 'string' ? d : 'Save failed')`.
